hyperedges.label: Canonical Per-Tick Data Flow

Model-Optional Task Routing

Model inference stack

smoke-selectors agent run: sequence of Pizzuno board observations

nodes.label: Internal canonical game state — not exposed as observation truth.
nodes.norm_label: internal canonical game state — not exposed as observation truth.

nodes.label: session-orchestrator
nodes.rationale: Central coordinator that manages session lifecycle, tick loop and recovery. Deliberately never interprets observations or reads entity state — it only calls plugins — so orchestration logic stays fully generic across games.

nodes.label: adapter-web
nodes.rationale: Primary path for browser games (DOM and canvas/WebGL). Never infers game state so the same adapter serves any game.

nodes.label: adapter-windows
nodes.rationale: Uses screenshot/vision/coordinate as the primary path because most desktop games render via DirectX/OpenGL/canvas and do not expose Windows UIA controls; screenshot capture works for any visible window.

nodes.label: uno-core
nodes.rationale: UNO was chosen as the first game plugin because it has clear visual elements, simple rules, runs in both browser DOM and canvas/WebGL (testing both adapter paths), and has a public web version (Pizzuno) for real-world testing. The architecture is not UNO-shaped.

nodes.label: svintus-core
nodes.rationale: Second game plugin that proves the multi-game plugin architecture works beyond UNO.

nodes.label: AdapterProtocol
nodes.rationale: Common protocol all adapters implement so the orchestrator dispatches through AdapterRegistry with zero adapter-type branching in core logic.

nodes.label: Canonical Pipeline (per tick)
nodes.rationale: Eight-stage tick: OBSERVE, INFER, LEGALIZE, DECIDE, PLAN, EXECUTE, VERIFY, RECORD. Uncertainty flows up through every layer to the orchestrator.

nodes.label: ObservedState
nodes.rationale: Raw UI detection with no game interpretation, so adapters stay generic and carry zero game knowledge.

nodes.label: LegalActions
nodes.rationale: Rules intersected with UI affordances (rules ∩ affordances) so the agent only considers moves that are both legal and actually clickable.

nodes.label: Uncertainty
nodes.rationale: Contradictions, missing fields and low-confidence values are appended by all layers and read by the orchestrator so uncertainty flows up rather than being silently dropped.

nodes.label: PerceptionPlugin
nodes.rationale: Converts raw observations into game-specific InferredState; may access VLM models but never touches UI execution, rules, or strategy.

nodes.label: RulesPlugin
nodes.rationale: Owns legal actions, turn detection and affordance reconciliation; never touches UI elements, DOM, selectors or coordinates.

nodes.label: StrategyPlugin
nodes.rationale: Chooses among legal actions; never knows about UI, element IDs or coordinates so strategy is decoupled from presentation.

nodes.label: ExecutionPlugin
nodes.rationale: The ONLY bridge that converts abstract game intent into concrete multi-step UI interaction plans with coordinates/clicks.

nodes.label: GameModelConfig
nodes.rationale: Purely declarative — a game plugin states preferred models per task; the platform resolves the route and the plugin never calls models directly.

nodes.label: ChatPolicy
nodes.rationale: Gates ALL chat responses (rate limiting, safety, directed-at-bot, operator override) so the bot never responds blindly and safety is not model-dependent.

nodes.label: ModelUsageTracker
nodes.rationale: Records every model invocation (task, provider, latency, fallback reason) so there are no silent failures and model behavior is transparent.

nodes.label: Model Fallback Chain
nodes.rationale: Preferred model → any matching use_case model → heuristic/template/rule-based → mock. Every fallback is logged; the pipeline never blocks on model availability because models are optional but first-class.

nodes.label: VLM Perception
nodes.rationale: Auto-triggered when DOM/UIA elements are unavailable (canvas/WebGL games) to extract structured state from screenshots; falls back to DOM-only.

nodes.label: ModelManifest
nodes.rationale: Model outputs are normalized to InferenceResponse and never applied to game state without policy-guard + uno-core validation.

nodes.label: Model Runtime Providers
nodes.rationale: mock, llama_cpp_openai and vllm_openai providers; model responses are advisory and never become uno-core truth.

nodes.label: Game Agent Platform
nodes.rationale: A human-in-the-loop universal game-playing agent platform. UNO is the first implemented plugin, not the architecture center; adding a game needs only new perception, rules, and optional strategy plugins while adapters/orchestrator/guard/UI stay generic.

nodes.label: Profile
nodes.rationale: Configuration for a specific game site: selectors, URLs, canvas coordinates. Lets adapter-web target a new game with no code change.

nodes.label: Profile Health Check
nodes.rationale: healthy/degraded/broken status from selector matching; sustained fallback ratio signals selector drift needing primary-selector updates before production reliance.

nodes.label: Full-Operator Evaluation
nodes.rationale: Runs in-process (backend not required) over OperatorScenario datasets to score legal_action_ok, policy_pass_ok and flow_complete_ok.

nodes.label: PlaywrightSession
nodes.rationale: DomSnapshot.extracted is adapter evidence, not canonical game state; UNO-specific interpretation happens in perception-service and uno-core.

nodes.label: PywinautoSession
nodes.rationale: uia backend preferred for modern apps, win32 fallback for legacy/sparse UIA; sparse trees lower confidence and mark sparse_tree=true.

nodes.label: chat-intent-service Client Integration
nodes.rationale: Chat is fully isolated from gameplay, so chat-intent detection can be safely integrated into any game client via POST /detect.

nodes.label: Windows Operator Client (Control Center)
nodes.rationale: Electron + React operator dashboard; first runs should use Assist mode (not Auto) for human-in-the-loop supervision.

nodes.label: Bounded Context Map (superseded)
nodes.rationale: Retained only for historical reference; superseded by overview.md which uses plugin-based naming where UNO is one implementation.

nodes.label: FlowController / State Machine
nodes.rationale: flow_controller.py, state_machine.py, recovery.py and clients.py are reusable with no UNO-specific imports in the state machine or recovery; never mutates canonical state directly and dedups execution via correlation_id.

nodes.label: Canvas games need web adapter, not windows adapter
nodes.norm_label: canvas games need web adapter, not windows adapter

nodes.label: Adapter outputs are evidence with confidence, never canonical game state
